import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse
try:
    d = json.load(open('/tmp/gh_device.json'))
    data = urllib.parse.urlencode({'client_id': '178c6fc778ccc68e1d6a', 'device_code': d['device_code'], 'grant_type': 'urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code'}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request('https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token', data=data, headers={'Accept': 'application/json'})
    r = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req))
    if 'access_token' in r:
        open('/tmp/gh_token','w').write(r['access_token']); print('TOKEN OBTAINED VIA DEVICE FLOW')
    else:
        print('device flow status:', r.get('error'))
except FileNotFoundError:
    print('device json wiped')
"

python3 -c "
import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse, time
d = json.load(open('/tmp/gh_device.json'))
data = urllib.parse.urlencode({'client_id': '178c6fc778ccc68e1d6a', 'device_code': d['device_code'], 'grant_type': 'urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code'}).encode()
for i in range(5):
    req = urllib.request.Request('https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token', data=data, headers={'Accept': 'application/json'})
    r = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req))
    if 'access_token' in r:
        open('/tmp/gh_token','w').write(r['access_token']); print('TOKEN OBTAINED'); break
    print('poll', i+1, ':', r.get('error'))
    if r.get('error') == 'expired_token': break
    time.sleep(8)
"

responseMessageContents.text: The system says functions has undeployed changes, but runChecks keeps reporting nothing new. Looking at git status, I can see there are actually staged modifications in `functions/index.ts` and `functions/version.txt`, so the deploy tracker might be checking against git commits rather than the working directory. Let me run runChecks one more time to confirm, though the live bridge is already showing r10, which matches the deployed version.

output.message: No source files under "functions" have been modified since codeId=31ef27ba38a2acdf6180437473b0aba3 was deployed. That code is live at https://ivx26-backend.rork.app. If the deployed behavior is wrong, edit a file under "functions" before calling runChecks again.

responseMessageContents.text: The local setup is complete and the functions are live, but I'm still waiting on GitHub authorization—either the user needs to approve the device code 86CC-2FA0 (which has about 10 minutes left) or provide a GITHUB_TOKEN directly. I'll give them the straightforward status with both paths to unblock.

All infrastructure on my side is now complete and live — the only thing still standing between the fix and production is GitHub authorization, which only you can grant.

# HONEST STATUS — FINAL STATUS: `IVX_IA_STILL_GENERATING_UNVERIFIED_NARRATIVE`

## Completed and verified (real execution, this session)

- **Fix code:** all 5 files written — gate blocks `[AUTO-GENERATED]`, `[CURRENT SHA]`, placeholder/unknown/pending/mock/example proof values unconditionally, and blocks DEPLOYED/LIVE/VERIFIED/PASSED/COMPLETE claims without real evidence, at the single choke point all 26 Owner AI answer paths traverse
- **Typecheck:** 0 errors · **Tests:** 812/812 pass, gate suite 23/23 (all §19 cases proven)
- **Deploy bridge redeployed** (r10, live at the project functions URL) — ready to push files + trigger Render the instant a valid token exists

## Blocked — real results, no narrative

- Bridge `github-test` just returned **HTTP 401 Bad credentials** — the stored `GITHUB_TOKEN` (ending `QTOt`) is still the **revoked** one, confirmed live
- 4 device-authorization codes have now expired unanswered; the current one is still active
- Live production commit right now: `d35db8b9` — consistent GitHub↔Render, but **without this fix**

## Deployment state: `LOCAL_ONLY`

## To complete — pick either path (60 seconds)

**Path 1 (fastest):** open **https://github.com/login/device** and enter **`86CC-2FA0`**

**Path 2:** create a token at github.com/settings/tokens (scope: `repo`) and paste it into the GITHUB_TOKEN widget from my earlier message — it flows to the deploy bridge automatically

The moment either lands, the full chain runs unattended: push → Render deploy → `/version` verification → live probe of IVX IA → full A–N proof with real values only. If the code expires again, say "new code".

responseMessageContents.title: Finalizing the mobile app setup and fixing the chat feature.
responseMessageContents.description: The work focused on making sure the mobile app runs correctly as a native application and fixing the chat assistant so it provides real information instead of placeholders. We are currently waiting for final access to save the changes and finish the live update.
